SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.annotations

Annotation Interface Failover

@Retention(RUNTIME) @Target(METHOD) @Documented public @interface Failover
Annotation for handling the failover of the invoking a method.

On successful method call, the result of invoking a method will be stored for recovery purpose. On failure method call, the result will be recovered if already exist in the store.

Each time an advised method is invoked, failover behavior will be applied, (store on success, recover on failure) for the method invoked for the given arguments.

A unique name must be provided for the failover.

The expiry of the stored data is managed based on the expiry duration and expiry unit given in the annotation. The default value of expiry duration is 1 and the default value of expiry unit is HOURS which is of type 'java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit'.

A sensible default simply uses the method parameters to compute the key or a custom 'com.societegenerale.failover.core.key.KeyGenerator' implementation can replace the default one (see keyGenerator()) by configuring the custom key generator bean name.

If no value is found in the failover store for the computed key, a null value will be returned and which can be handled by providing a 'com.societegenerale.failover.core.payload.RecoveredPayloadHandler'.

Since:
1.0.0
Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Required Element Summary

    Required Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Required Element
    Description
    String
    name
    The name of the failover.

  • Optional Element Summary

    Optional Elements
    Modifier and Type
    Optional Element
    Description
    String
    domain
    Optional logical domain for store partitioning.
    long
    expiryDuration
    The expiry duration for computing the expiry of the entities stored in the failover store.
    String
    expiryDurationExpression
    The expiry duration expression for computing the expiry of the entities stored in the failover store.
    String
    expiryPolicy
    The bean name of the custom ExpiryPolicy to use.
    ChronoUnit
    expiryUnit
    The expiry unit for computing the expiry of the entities stored in the failover store.
    String
    expiryUnitExpression
    The expiry unit expression for computing the expiry of the entities stored in the failover store.
    String
    keyGenerator
    The bean name of the custom KeyGenerator to use.
    String
    payloadSplitter
    The bean name of the custom PayloadSplitter to use for scatter/gather mode.
    boolean
    recoverAll
    Forces recover-all mode even when the method is called with non-empty (ID) arguments.

  • Element Details

    • name

      String name
      The name of the failover. A unique name must be provided.
      Returns:
      name of the failover

    • expiryDuration

      long expiryDuration
      The expiry duration for computing the expiry of the entities stored in the failover store. The default value is 1.
      Returns:
      expiry duration of the failover
      Default:
      1L

    • expiryDurationExpression

      String expiryDurationExpression
      The expiry duration expression for computing the expiry of the entities stored in the failover store. If you specify expression it will be taken for computing expiry otherwise expiryDuration will be taken. The default value is "".
      Returns:
      expiry duration expression of the failover
      Default:
      ""

    • expiryUnit

      ChronoUnit expiryUnit
      The expiry unit for computing the expiry of the entities stored in the failover store. See ChronoUnit. The default value is ChronoUnit.HOURS.
      Returns:
      expiry unit of the failover
      Default:
      HOURS

    • expiryUnitExpression

      String expiryUnitExpression
      The expiry unit expression for computing the expiry of the entities stored in the failover store. If you specify expression it will be taken for computing expiry otherwise expiryUnit will be taken. The default value is "".
      Returns:
      expiry unit expression of the failover
      Default:
      ""

    • keyGenerator

      String keyGenerator
      The bean name of the custom KeyGenerator to use. If not configured, the default key generator will be used.
      Returns:
      key generator bean name
      Default:
      ""

    • expiryPolicy

      String expiryPolicy
      The bean name of the custom ExpiryPolicy to use. If not configured, the default expiry policy will be used.
      Returns:
      expiry policy bean name
      Default:
      ""

    • payloadSplitter

      String payloadSplitter
      The bean name of the custom PayloadSplitter to use for scatter/gather mode.

      When set, enables scatter/gather mode:

      • Store path: the composite method result is split into individual per-entity slices via PayloadSplitter#splitOnStore, each stored under its own key.
      • Recover path: per-slice keys are derived via PayloadSplitter#splitOnRecover and looked up independently; available slices are merged via PayloadSplitter#merge. Partial recovery is handled gracefully.

      If empty (default), standard single-key behaviour applies.

      Returns:
      payload splitter bean name
      Default:
      ""

    • domain

      String domain
      Optional logical domain for store partitioning.

      When set, this value is used as the store namespace instead of name(), and as the prefix for key generation. Two @Failover annotations with the same domain share the same store entries — a successful call on either populates data the other can recover from.

      Typical use: a single-entity endpoint and a scatter/gather list endpoint covering the same business entity type (e.g. domain = "country").

      Expiry consistency: failovers sharing a domain must use the same expiry configuration. When expiry differs, the last writer overwrites the stored expiry timestamp for that entry. The scanner warns at startup when mismatched expiry is detected within a domain.

      When empty (default), name() is used — existing behavior is preserved.

      Returns:
      domain name, or empty string to default to name()
      See Also:
      • name()
      Default:
      ""

    • recoverAll

      boolean recoverAll
      Forces recover-all mode even when the method is called with non-empty (ID) arguments.

      Recover-all retrieves every stored entry for the failover's referential name (via the store's findAll) instead of a single key lookup.

      Trigger precedence: recover-all is performed when either the method arguments are null/empty (no ID args) or this flag is true. Setting recoverAll = true is therefore only needed to force recover-all for a method that does receive arguments; with no arguments, recover-all already applies and this flag is redundant.

      A payloadSplitter() must be configured in both cases.

      Returns:
      true to force recover-all regardless of arguments; false (default) to recover-all only when no ID arguments are supplied
      See Also:
      • payloadSplitter()
      Default:
      false
